\title{A Minimal Reproducibility Test Paper}
\author{Test Author}
\begin{document}
\maketitle

\section{Introduction}
We propose a Mask Network for identifying critical states in policy refinement.
Our refinement method achieves a strictly larger improvement in cumulative
reward than JSRL and Random baselines on the selfish mining environment.

\section{Method}
The MaskNetwork class outputs 0 for inputs identified as critical states and 1
for non-critical states. We refine only the actions corresponding to critical
states, leaving non-critical actions untouched.

\section{Experiments}
We measure cumulative reward before and after refinement using JSRL.
Experiment II runs the selfish mining environment with the JSRL refinement
method, producing pre-refinement and post-refinement cumulative reward
measurements.

\section{Results}
The 'Ours' refinement method brings the largest improvement to the pretrained
policy network. Across 5 seeds, Ours improves cumulative reward by 23.4\%,
while JSRL achieves 14.1\% and Random achieves 6.2\%.

\section{Conclusion}
The proposed method outperforms baselines on cumulative reward improvement.
